centerpiece.heading: Main Work
centerpiece.title: Cryptionary
centerpiece.description: This project is a dynamic cryptocurrency tracking website developed using React.js and TailwindCSS. It's an actively evolving project with a roadmap for incorporating advanced features and styles.

1.title: Data Integration
1.body: Leverages APIs from reputable sources like CoinGecko and Coinpaprika to fetch real-time cryptocurrency data, including prices, market capitalization, and trading volumes.

2.title: Interactive UI/UX
2.body: Implements engaging user interface elements such as animated gradient transitions, a captivating hover effect with mouse tracking text, and a smooth sliding sidebar for enhanced navigation and user experience.

3.title: Resilience in Restricted Environments
3.body: Designed to function effectively despite potential internet sanctions. To demonstrate full functionality, static data is utilized, ensuring all features are showcased even when live data fetching is not feasible.

about.title: About Me

stack1.title: Core stack
stack1.text: React, Next.js, Tailwind CSS, TypeScript, JavaScript (ES6+), CSS3, HTML5

stack2.title: Familiar with
stack2.text: Database fundimentals, Figma, Git/GitHub, Vercel , cPanel, DNS & deployment workflows, AI tools

about.body1: Based in Tehran, Iran — fully fluent in English (written and spoken).
about.body2: I'm looking for a team where I can apply what I already know, learn from experienced engineers, and contribute to products that solve real problems.
about.body3: If you'd like to discuss an opportunity or collaborate, feel free to get in touch.

myStory.1: I was 25 when I became a teacher, driven by a genuine desire to make an impact. Shortly after, I confronted a hard truth:
myStory.2: Teaching is an honorable job, but it has a ceiling. the structure is simple: time for money - indefinitely. In this model, hard work and dedication yields linear returns — never exponential.
myStory.3: So I turned inward. I analyzed my personality, long-term vision, and reached some clarity. My mind craves complexity and meaningful challenges. I prefer ambitious, high-upside work over settling for a quiet, comfortable path.
myStory.4: During that search, I kept bumping into the same idea:
myStory.6: I dove into front-end development headfirst. The fundamentals clicked quickly, but soon I realized these were just the alphabet. So I went further. Deeper.
myStory.7: My English fluency gave me direct access to top-tier documentations, courses, and resources across the world. I absorbed them quickly. I still do.
myStory.8: Moving from “learning” to “qualified” was a three-year marathon of self-discipline — funded by a second job and fueled by a long-term vision. When progress was invisible and doubt kept creeping in, I stayed consistent. I kept building long before the rewards showed up.
myStory.9: Real client work shaped my perspective. It taught me to ship, to communicate, and to take responsibility for outcomes. I find it deeply satisfying to take something abstract — a design, a concept, a rough vision — and turn it into something real, usable, and enjoyable, all through
myStory.10: I wanted a craft, not just a job — one where every month, I’d be better than the last. That’s what drew me to front-end and often keeps me at my desk longer than I plan.
myStory.11: I want a team where pushing each other forward is built into the culture — not just written on a wall.
myStory.12: I got this far on my own. It taught me discipline and resilience. Now I'm ready to invest that discipline into a team with a goal worth fighting for.
